# Allowlist for `tools/check_stale_terms` (a.k.a. `task lint:stale-terms`).
#
# Format: one path per line, optionally with `:N-M` to restrict the
# allowlist to lines [N..M] inclusive of that file. A path WITHOUT a
# range whitelists the entire file (use sparingly: line ranges are
# preferred so a regression elsewhere in the file still surfaces).
# Lines starting with `#` are comments. Path separators are
# forward-slashes regardless of host OS.
#
# Owner: `.cursor/plans/migration-cleanup-docs.plan.md` (plan 17).
# Adding a new entry to this file means you intentionally retained
# a retired term as historical context. Update the matching `History`
# section so a reader landing on the line knows why the stale term
# is still there.

# --- ROADMAP and policy doc History sections ---------------------------
# ROADMAP.md "History" section explains the ReferenceImpl + FallbackEngine
# layout the multi-strategy coordinator replaces. Keep the section anchored
# so the future "why was the engine refactored" question has an answer.
ROADMAP.md:93-104

# ROADMAP.md "Execution plans" History sub-section explains the retired
# DuckDB-only transpiler plan set whose accomplishments carried forward.
ROADMAP.md:38-49

# `docs/ENGINE_POLICY.md` "History" section is the canonical narrative
# for the runtimes the local coordinator replaces -- both the
# ReferenceImpl + FallbackEngine pair and the brief DuckDB-only era.
docs/ENGINE_POLICY.md:192-225

# Active rule prose intentionally references `FallbackEngine` so a reader
# can map the rule to the historical hazard it prevents.
docs/ENGINE_POLICY.md:60-80

# --- Plan files: each plan's Background section explains the problem
# ---  the new coordinator solves, which means it names the retired
# ---  vocabulary one or more times. Plan files are documentation
# ---  artifacts; whitelisted whole-file because their `Background`
# ---  paragraphs are the reason they exist.
.cursor/plans/local-execution-roadmap-index.plan.md
.cursor/plans/migration-cleanup-docs.plan.md
.cursor/plans/local-exec-00-index.plan.md
.cursor/plans/engine-router-foundation.plan.md

# --- README has one stale term inside an active prose paragraph that
# ---  describes a historical removal: the "removed when the
# ---  ReferenceImpl engine and in-memory storage backends were deleted"
# ---  paragraph. Cover the matching prose block.
README.md:399-415

# --- ROADMAP.md "Distribution / Runtime configuration" paragraph
# ---  explains why `--engine` / `--storage` / `--on_unknown_fn` flags
# ---  no longer exist. The `ReferenceImpl` reference is intrinsic
# ---  to that explanation.
ROADMAP.md:650-665

# --- ENGINE_POLICY.md leading "Runtime" paragraph names the removed
# ---  ReferenceImpl flag set so a reader landing on the doc knows
# ---  why the flag surface is single-knob today.
docs/ENGINE_POLICY.md:33-43

# --- dist/CHANGELOG.md is an auto-generated release-notes artifact;
# ---  the historical commit messages it embeds reference the
# ---  removed FallbackEngine plumbing verbatim. Whitelisted whole
# ---  because future regenerations would otherwise churn the line
# ---  numbers in this allowlist.
dist/CHANGELOG.md

# --- The lint:stale-terms task description in taskfiles/lint.yml
# ---  intentionally enumerates the banned phrases it polices. Cover
# ---  the matching `desc:` block so a future addition to the linter
# ---  vocabulary doesn't have to fight this allowlist.
taskfiles/lint.yml:197-205

# --- Coordinator + classifier headers reference `FallbackEngine` to
# ---  explain what hazard the route classifier replaces. The active
# ---  rule wording deliberately retains the historical name.
backend/engine/coordinator/route_classifier.h:40-50
